text: Agi Yesu as…õ niŐĀ lin…Ēkpi le su i, atr…õ niŐĀ kepea m…õ, l…õ y…õ bad…Ēkpasi ba b…õba y…õ kl…Ē l…õ b…õsi y…õ si, ‚ÄúBu abee niŐĀ siwa s…õ niŐĀ …Ēnyr…Ē n…Ē m…õ su ese ki bl…Ē.‚ÄĚ
translated: Jesus told them this parable, saying, "A sower went out to sow, and when he sowed, some seeds fell along the path, and the birds came and devoured them. Similarly, when they hear the word and do not understand it, the evil one comes and takes away what was sown in their hearts."

ref: After Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone into the house, they asked him what these sayings meant.
text: Gi as…õ niŐĀ lin…Ēkpi le su xe atr…õ niŐĀ kepea m…õ …õ, y…õ bad…Ēkpasi ba be či ye si obu abee li…õ ya ese …Ēki ba.
translated: I speak words that do no evil; listen to me, those who come will do their ancestors' good work in the town.
